title: Metric Dashboards for Agents
concept: Monitoring key metrics: average steps per task, token count per success, tool error rate, and user feedback.

exercises.prompt: What tool helps visualize traces in complex, multi-agent runs?
exercises.explain: LangSmith is a popular tool for visualizing traces to debug multi-agent processes.

cards.title: What Are Metric Dashboards?
cards.body: Metric dashboards display important measurements about agent performance so we can understand how well they are working.

cards.title: Key Metrics to Monitor
cards.body: Four important metrics to track include average steps per task, token count per success, tool error rate, and user feedback.

cards.title: Metrics as a Dashboard
cards.body: Think of metrics like a car's dashboard, showing speed, fuel level, and engine health. They help you know how well your engine (agent) is performing.

cards.title: Understanding Average Steps per Task
cards.body: If an agent takes an average of 5 steps to complete a task, we want to see if that's good or if it can be improved.

cards.title: Myth: All Metrics are the Same
cards.body: Not all metrics are equally important. Knowing the right ones helps focus on what truly matters for agent performance.

cards.title: Comparing Metrics
cards.body: Here’s how two key metrics compare:

exercises.prompt: Which metric tracks how many times an agent makes mistakes?
exercises.explain: Tool error rate measures how often an agent encounters errors, which is crucial for understanding its reliability.

exercises.prompt: Average steps per task is a key metric for understanding user feedback.
exercises.explain: Average steps per task measures the efficiency of the agent, while user feedback evaluates user satisfaction.

exercises.prompt: Tracking the ________ helps understand how many tasks an agent completes successfully.
exercises.sentence: Tracking the token count per success helps understand how many tasks an agent completes successfully.
exercises.explain: Token count per success indicates the effectiveness of the agent in relation to the tasks it completes.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is NOT a key metric for agents?
exercises.explain: Fuel efficiency is not a relevant metric for monitoring AI agents’ performance; the other two are key metrics we track.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to monitor tool error rate and how does it relate to user feedback?

options.text: It helps improve error handling, which can lead to better user feedback.
It has no impact on user feedback.
It's only relevant for financial metrics.
It only matters in the testing phase.

exercises.explain: Monitoring tool error rates allows us to identify issues and enhance agents, ultimately leading to improved user satisfaction and feedback.
